page: Amazon Web Services m8i.24xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs, consisting of 48 physical cores with 2 threads per core, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The underlying processor is an Intel Xeon 6975P-C running at 3.9 GHz, equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache. The system is configured with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple domains. The instance achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core and multi-core metrics, Passmark CPU scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, ranking in the top 10% for both cached and uncached read/write operations. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are enhanced by hardware flags such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). In database and web serving tests, the instance demonstrates high throughput, registering 172.76 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 155.47 GB/sec in static web serving.

While the m8i.24xlarge delivers top-tier prompt processing speeds for large language models, its text generation speeds for 135M and 70B models fall into the average category, highlighting a performance tradeoff in sustained generative tasks. The lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. Qualitatively, the high-density DDR5 memory and dedicated Intel Xeon cores offer strong resource efficiency for enterprise applications. This instance is well-suited for software compilation, database operations, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, it utilizes the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but provides a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place the m8i.24xlarge in the top tier for memory bandwidth, Geekbench multi-core performance, and Passmark database operations. It also exhibits strong cryptographic and vector processing capabilities via AVX-512 and Intel AMX extensions. However, a performance tradeoff is observed in LLM text generation, which registers average speeds compared to its top-tier prompt processing capabilities. This instance is highly suited for enterprise database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier database, memory bandwidth, and CPU benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier database and memory performance.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and top-tier multi-core and database performance.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 3.9 GHz clock speed and 480 MB L3 cache
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmarks
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Suited for enterprise databases, software compilation, and web serving
